comments.comment_body:
Really insightful post! I like the way you broke down the chain-of-though process- you made it clear that even complex AI reasoning can be approached step by step. Your clear explanations not only shed light on the nuances between the two methods but also highlight the potential for more reasoning in AI.
It’s inspiring to see such thorough analysis that make complex ideas accessible.
Keep up the great work, your insights are sparking brilliant discussions and creativity.
comments.post_body:
🎉 Thrilled to share that my article on Chain-of-Thought Prompting has been featured as a HackerNoon Top Story!

https://lnkd.in/gTraFTWx

In this piece, I break down how Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompting is revolutionizing the way AI models approach complex problem-solving. Just like teaching a child step-by-step, this technique helps Large Language Models develop more human-like reasoning abilities.

Key highlights:
1. Detailed comparison between standard prompting vs CoT prompting
2. Real-world applications in solving mathematical puzzles
3. Introduction to Zero-Shot Chain-of-Thought concept
4. The future of multimodal Chain-of-Thought

Special thanks to the HackerNoon community for the warm reception! If you're interested in AI prompting techniques and making AI think more systematically, check out the full article here: https://lnkd.in/gNaM9znx

#AI #MachineLearning #PromptEngineering #Tech #HackerNoon #ChainOfThought
comments.context_summary: The commenter is responding to a post about an article on Chain-of-Thought Prompting being featured as a HackerNoon Top Story, which discusses how this technique is revolutionizing AI problem-solving by enabling more human-like reasoning abilities. The article explores the benefits and applications of Chain-of-Thought prompting in AI models.

comments.comment_body: Thank you Aqib Hussain Malik. Glad you found the article useful.

comments.comment_body: Taha Hussain : invisible engineers often find peace.
comments.post_body:
Some engineers I worked with at Microsoft never got promoted.

They weren’t political.
They weren’t toxic.
They just made one fatal mistake:

They assumed great code = great career.

But code doesn’t advocate for you in calibration meetings.

People do.

Your promo packet isn’t just about impact.

It’s about perceived impact, narrated clearly, backed by people who matter.

If you can’t speak business,
If you can’t explain your work without using acronyms,
If no one outside your team knows your name…

You’re invisible.

And invisible engineers don’t get promoted.

If you’re ready to grow beyond titles and start influencing, 
this is where it begins ↓

https://lnkd.in/gMGfmVas

Stop chasing titles. 
Start attracting roles.
comments.context_summary: The post discusses how great coding skills alone are not enough for career advancement, and that engineers need to be able to communicate their impact and build relationships to get promoted. The commenter is responding to the post's argument that technical ability is not the only factor in career success.

comments.post_body:
Delhivery I'm in dire need of help! 

I urgently want my shipment to be delivered as my dad is physically disabled and the boxes have his artificial limb. I have been following up for more than 14 days now, my dad is not able to walk properly and is constantly suffering at the hands of Delhivery.

I engaged DTDC packers and movers to ship my stuff from Bangalore to Delhi on 27 July 2022 and paid them the full amount for the shipment. However, DTDC Packers and Movers engaged Delhivery for the shipment.

On 4th August, the consignment was brought to my location in Delhi and was taken back because of some payment issue. On enquiry, I got to know that the DTDC Packers and movers didn't pay Delhivery and when I was trying to talk to them to make the payment, the delivery guy asked me to sort the payment issue and he will deliver it tomorrow i.e. 5th August. He gave me his number and asked me to call him to deliver it. Further, to my utter shock the Delhivery marked my consignment status as "Refused to Accept".

Upon multiple follow ups, I figured out that DTDC packers and movers had no intention of paying Delhivery. 
Next day i.e. 6th August, I called the delivery guy to deliver the shipment and assured him I'll pay Delhivery for the FOD charges because DTDC Packers and Movers refused to pay and I was in dire need of my consignment. 

I have also raised a ticket (Ticket no - 44251455) on the Delhivery website, followed up a number of times with the Delhivery guys and still I am waiting for my shipment to be delivered.

I was told by the Delhivery team that the person who sent my shipment has put the delivery on hold and hence they will not be able to deliver it. I struggled and asked DTDC packers and movers to remove the hold, they have confirmed they have sent an email regarding the same to Delhivery.

It's been more than 14 days now and I am still following up to get my shipment back even when I am ready to pay the due amount. My dad is a senior citizen and is in trouble and I need help to get my shipment delivered ASAP.

The LR number is 231357428.

Need your immediate intervention in solving this as my father is suffering due to Delhivery's unresponsiveness.

Bhavesh Manglani Suraj Saharan Aamir Bhat Ritesh Kumar Ashish Dhamija Sandeep Barasia Kapil Bharati Ajith Pai Amit Agarwal Mohit Tandon Kavya Shetty Gurvinder Singh Varun Chaudhari Jidesh Haridas Harshit Gangwar Could please help me here? It's a humble request.

Please note: It is not DTDC Express Limited but DTDC packers and movers. Please let not tag incorrect company.

#delhivery
comments.context_summary: The commenter is urgently seeking help with a delayed shipment from Delhivery, which contains their father's artificial limb, and has been held up due to a payment issue between Delhivery and DTDC Packers and Movers. The commenter has been following up for over 14 days and is willing to pay the due amount to get the shipment delivered.

comments.post_body: This is a bittersweet moment for me, and I want to start with a BIG THANK YOU! After ~9.5 years at Microsoft, I am planning on taking next steps in my career. Microsoft is an amazing company and will always have a special place in my heart. I joined Microsoft back in 2013 after completing my undergrad and I could not have asked for a better place to begin my career than at Microsoft – a place that has always felt like home! Regardless of the org, team, manager, etc. one thing that was consistent was Microsoft’s values and culture which I deeply adored! There are countless number of colleagues, manager, mentors, executives who helped me along the journey to become who I am today (and more), and I will be forever grateful to each and every one of you. THANK YOU again Microsoft and I wish my Alma Mater continued success and glory for years to come! Super excited for what's next to come! #microsoft #thankyou #grateful
comments.context_summary: The commenter is responding to a post about someone leaving Microsoft after 9.5 years, expressing gratitude for their time at the company and announcing their next career steps. The commenter is likely offering congratulations or well wishes to the departing employee.

comments.post_body:
I brought this to Dubai along with me... 

This is Samovar, a Kashmiri variant of a kettle with hand engraved motifs which represent the cultural landscape of the valley. In it is a chamber of fire, which is home to burning coals which heats up the entire kettle from the inside. There is another chamber surrounding the fire in which all the ingredients to brew the perfect tea are added. The strength of the Copper maintains the high temperature which is required to extract the complete essence of your divine drinks. This little samovar can also be used as a piece of home décor.
comments.context_summary: The commenter is responding to a post about a Samovar, a traditional Kashmiri kettle with hand-engraved motifs, that the author brought to Dubai and is describing its unique features and uses. The commenter is likely to share their thoughts or experiences related to this cultural artifact or tea-brewing device.

comments.comment_body: Gaurav Sen : Work life balance and flexibility !
comments.post_body:
Have multiple offers? 
Your team(1), role(2), and project(3) are important.
Money(4) is also a factor.


Company brand(5) is a distant fifth.
--------

Recently, I've seen folks join Google and Microsoft for lesser pay and a lower position (SDE-2 to SDE-1). 
Just because they want to associate with the brand.

What the hell is going on lol. 

Brands come and go. The brand you'll be associated with the longest is YOURSELF. Choose what's best for you.

#Career #Branding #LongTerm
comments.context_summary: The commenter is responding to a post about prioritizing factors when considering multiple job offers, emphasizing that personal brand and long-term goals are more important than company brand. They express surprise that some individuals choose to join big companies like Google and Microsoft for lesser pay and position, just for the brand association.

comments.comment_body:
I have seen HR offer 50% more than what candidate asked .
Let’s say candidate was at 9Lpa and asked for 12Lpa the HR offered 18Lpa .
comments.post_body:
HRs on LinkedIn  -
"The candidate asked for 50k, but I offered him 80k as the budget allowed me to do that"

HRs in real life - 
The candidate asked for 50k, but I offered him 45k even though the budget allowed for 80k - just for the sake of negotiation

Apart from LinkedIn posts, I have never seen HRs offer more than the candidate has quoted.

#hiring #humanresources
comments.context_summary: The post discusses the difference between how HRs present themselves on LinkedIn versus real life, specifically in regards to salary negotiations, where they often lowball candidates despite having a higher budget. The commenter is responding to the original post highlighting this disparity in HR behavior.

comments.post_body:
Today is a tough day in my career. All six universities I applied to for my Computer Science undergraduate (Massachusetts Institute of Technology, Stanford University, Harvard University, New York University, Caltech, and Tufts University) rejected me. I think it is important to share content like this too because life is not always great and it has highs and lows.

I worked over 300 hours on my applications. This process including the application fees and the required tests cost around $1,000.

I will see what the future brings. I still want to go to the US – maybe with the help of a company that offers me a job there or maybe as a transfer student. The dots will connect, but I'll have to wait for them.

Keep in mind to always stay positive and go your way no matter what happens!

#motivation #growth #career #positivity #university #admissions #us  #studyabroad #international #future #education #universities #student  #technology
comments.context_summary: The author is sharing their disappointment after being rejected by six top universities for their Computer Science undergraduate program, despite putting in over 300 hours of work and spending $1,000 on the application process. They're choosing to stay positive and look forward to alternative paths to achieving their goals, such as working with a company or transferring as a student.

comments.comment_body: Hard work and determination pays off eventually !
comments.post_body:
300+ rejections to working at Google, Microsoft, Tesla and a startup in a year (as an international student).

These are the things that worked for me:

- Improved my skills and resume
- Applied early (first day of application opening)
- Got referrals
- Attended conferences
- Messaged people on LinkedIn for advice, referrals and mock interviews 
- Prepared well for interviews
- Did a lot of mocks (for PM)
- Stayed consistent. This is the most important. Keep applying, keep improving your skills.

Nope, I did not hit the lottery in a year. It took 2.5 years of consistent rejections to reiterate and get better. So don’t get demotivated seeing others, your turn will come :) You do you.

I’ve been off LinkedIn for a while but I’m back to post more resources. Next post with detailed resources I used to prep🤞 

Meanwhile, check out Scholarship Track.

#internships
comments.context_summary: The commenter is responding to a post about overcoming 300+ rejections from top companies, sharing their own experience of persistence and improvement, and offering advice to stay motivated and keep applying. The original post details the strategies that ultimately led to the author's success after 2.5 years of consistent rejections.

comments.comment_body:

comments.post_body:
I am extremely disappointed and angry with #tatagroup  #tatamotors for FALSELY ADVERTISING the “Altroz” as one of the safest if not the safest car in India and putting me and my family’s life at HUGE risk.  I have owned the Tata Altroz Petrol since Feb 2020. Please hear my story and think twice before buying a Tata car.
My mother and I drove down to Goa on 18-Dec-2021 for a family vacation in my Tata Altroz. On 25-Dec-2021 while I was driving back home to my hotel, I met with an accident during which I lost control of the car and banged with significant force into an electricity pole. The front of the car was badly damaged due to the impact (see attached photo) but THE FRONT DRIVER AND PASSENGER AIRBAGS DID NOT OPEN (see attached photo). Fortunately, I had my seatbelt on and miraculously survived with some scratches and a badly bruised knee. I am also extremely lucky that my mother was not present in the car at that time! I had to get my car towed to SP Cars (Tata Motors), Verna, Goa where it is awaiting assessment and repairs.
What I find simply unacceptable is that a car that @tatamotors claims to be so safe (constantly marketing and advertising its Global NCAP 5-star rating to gullible customers) DID NOT HAVE FUNCTIONING AIRBAGS, one of the most basic safety features mandated by the authorities. I sincerely request the Ministry Of Road #morth @Ministry of Road Transport and Highways, Government of India to take appropriate action as well.
It is safe to say that I will be selling my car as soon as possible and will never purchase another vehicle from this brand again. I also urge everyone seeing this to avoid the brand as much as possible and not fall for their false promises or lies. Deeply disappointed with #TataMotors as a proud Indian and a long-time customer.

#india #safety #carsafety #transport #Morthindia

After 17 years in Microsoft, working on Windows, Office and lately in Azure with awesome people and exciting projects, I’m humbled and super excited to share that I’m starting a new position as Engineering Manager at Meta!

#microsoft #meta
What an excellent experience it has been building this beautiful play space for children in Kashmir with the wonderful team of Anthill Creations Pooja Rai Sreejith Ajith
Happy New Year to all of you .. May you all sleep 8 hours every day this year ❤️

If your Google Chrome freezes ever,  hit shift+esc. Chrome has an inbuilt task manager.

comments.comment_body:
The soldiers live for keeping the nation safe and sacrificing their lives for their homeland and they are not concerned about media attention, the other story is the media love story.
As far as the media is concerned they are a TRP based entertainment cum news industry so can’t really expect them to align fully to our  expectations or to address all our concerning news.
comments.post_body:
Yesterday, 

One 23 yr old sacrificed his precious life for our motherland. Our young Army officer Lt #RishiKumar & Sepoy #ManjitSingh lost their precious lives in an Improvised Explosive Devise (IED) blast near a forward post along the Line of Control (LoC) in Nowshera sector of Jammu and Kashmir’s Rajouri district on Saturday.

जब देश में थी दीवाली ,वोह खेल रहे थे होली 
जब हम बैठे थे घरों में, वोह झेल रहे थे गोली l

One 23 yr old super rich guy (from a privileged family, courtesy Indians who support mediocre quality escapist-puppy love popcorn films) came back home on bail, with full-fanfare, after 20+ days long existence in a jail, in a drug case. The welcome given to this guy was loud and celebratory as if he had won the Kargil war. Most of us were appalled to see the crowds gathered here.

Guess, who did the Indian media highlight as the headliner? The latter. Who did Indians like us thought of and extended our prayers to? The former. While the media in India busy eulogizing the 23 yr drug accused son of a Bollywood star, most of us were sad for our martyrs, the 23 yr old Lieutenant and the sepoy; we have long been feeling quite uncomfortable with the loss of young patriotic Indians at the borders, on a regular basis. 

Besides this, I am sure, most of us are bothered by something : the Indian media (which still has the money bags and reach to influence young and old minds) and their blatant preference for rich and famous, divisive and wokes, and their incapacity to regard our soldiers, to raise debates/discussions on every crucial issue  - from the still under-developed primary education facilities in the country to child-trafficking to lack of proper roads in the remotest parts of India where teachers cover miles & cross rivers carrying mid-day meals ration for their school children to everything that needs a rapid attention. 

See, most of us could share, raise discussions on some issues, but we cannot match their reach on influencing people. And journalism is not our profession. We have our respective career and related responsibilities to chase. We will continue to do our bit to raise awareness about various issues, the rising wokeism for which a good chunk of urban young and professionals are falling for, and about the most pressing need to stay United as the country etc etc. But I wonder, is this enough. Can we survive against the deep-pocketed biased machinery that tends to pitch the rich-powerful guys as the representatives of the then our rich-but savagely plundered civilization-but now resurgent young economy, while ignoring the real Indians, real unsung heroes who deserve to be the headlines? I am leaving the link for the article on the superstar, for you all to read : 

‘Shahrukh Khan was India...’

https://lnkd.in/gucQQRnq

Really? Is our India-her culture that small to be equated with a star?

Yup, Its a Wrap !
 
After 7.5 amazing years my role at VMware will be ending tomorrow
my new venture which starts post a week of party and vacation 😊
 
It felt like being a part of a blockbuster,
which had everything from cultural diversity,
friendship, joy, celebration, resilience and success.
I am sad leaving a team and company I loved to work with
but also feel happy with what we all have achieved together. #LWD #vmware #teamawesome   #work

Highway construction in India hits a record pace!

Despite COVID induced lockdowns, India has constructed 13,298 km of highways in 2020-21 with a pace of 36.5 KM/day.

India's aim is to touch upto 100 km/day!

Always more about how you do your job than what you have got to do it with...
